channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colorado woman ID'ed as driver in crash that killed officer
item.description: The driver of a vehicle that collided head on with a Navasota police vehicle killing Sgt. Mark Butler has been identified as 59-year-old Kimberly Lannigan, of Colorado Springs, Colorado. She was also killed in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Roland Richter, Fox 5

item.title: US border patrol agent was fatally shot in Vermont near Canada
item.description: Authorities on Tuesday were investigating a fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent that also left a suspect dead and another injured on a Vermont highway near Canada, authorities said. Agent David Maland was killed Monday afternoon following a traffic stop, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A frigid storm drops rare snow on Houston and New Orleans as Florida readies plows in the Panhandle
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A rare frigid storm charged through Texas and the northern Gulf Coast on Tuesday, blanketing New Orleans and Houston with snow that closed highways, grounded nearly all flights and canceled school for more than a million students more accustomed to hurricane dismissals than snow d
item.dc:creator: GERALD HERBERT and DAVID J. PHILLIP, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California officials reopen certain Palisades Fire zones, cops to use planes to monitor looting
item.description: California officials will reopen some Palisades Fire evacuation zones, as law enforcement ramps up security to address looting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth's former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators vetting the nomination of Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump defends pardoning of violent Jan. 6 offenders
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday defended signing mass pardons for rioters charged in connection with the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack, including those who were violent offenders, a move that has drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Warnings for dangerous winds extended in Southern California as new wildfires break out
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Forecasters warned that dangerous winds will buffet Southern California for at least two more days as new wildfires broke out on Tuesday, a pair of major Los Angeles-area blazes burned for a third week, and officials made preparations to protect scorched neighborhoods from toxic a
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Guitarist John Sykes of Whitesnake, Thin Lizzy dies
item.description: "In his final days, he spoke of his sincere love and gratitude for his fans who stuck by him through all these years," a statement released Monday read.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires heads of TSA, Coast Guard and guts key aviation safety advisory committee
item.description: President Donald Trump has moved quickly to remake the Department of Homeland Security by firing the heads of the Transportation Security Administration and Coast Guard before their terms are up while eliminating all the members of a key aviation security advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Residents ordered to pay $850,000 bond to appeal Ohio derailment settlement vow not to give up
item.description: The residents who question whether Norfolk Southern's $600 million settlement offers enough compensation for the East Palestine train crash don't plan to let a judge's order requiring an $850,000 bond derail their appeal.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Elephants can't pursue their release from a Colorado zoo because they're not human, court says
item.description: Five elephants at a Colorado zoo may be “majestic” but do not have the legal right to pursue their release since they aren't human.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Slevin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's first day back in White House includes firings, infrastructure announcement
item.description: President Donald Trump is spending his first full day back in the White House meeting with congressional leaders, making an infrastructure announcement and demonstrating one of his favored expressions of power: firing people.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, KTLA 5

item.title: American deputy killed by stray bullet on Turks and Caicos vacation
item.description: Illinois sheriff's deputy Shamone Duncan was killed alongside a local resident while on vacation in Turks and Caicos for her sister's birthday this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Truck stop serial killer to go on trial for 2007 murder of Indianapolis woman
item.description: Carma Purpura disappeared from the Flying J truck stop outsude Indianapolis and wasn't seen again until her body was found along an interstate in Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Max Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted by Trump administration with no explanation
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KTLA 5

item.title: Garth Hudson, The Band's last surviving member, dies at 87
item.description: Garth Hudson, the Band's virtuoso keyboardist and all-around musician who drew from a unique palette of sounds and styles to add a conversational touch to such rock standards as "Up on Cripple Creek," “The Weight” and "Rag Mama Rag," has died at age 87.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agent killed in Vermont identified
item.description: A Customs and Border Protection source has identified the Border Patrol agent shot and killed during a traffic stop Monday as David Maland, 44.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sting snares armed felon smugglers tied to foreign prison gang, cartel: police
item.description: The Texas Department of Public Safety said it nabbed three ex-convicts with weapons and drugs in El Paso on Friday, including one admitted human smuggler with the Paisa prison gang.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thieves steal over $1K worth of meat from Walmart: Police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Two men stole over $1,200 worth of meat products from a Walmart in the Hickory Hill area of Memphis, Tennessee, on Saturday, Memphis Police reported. Officers responded to the shoplifting call at Walmart on 2856 Hickory Hill Road.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: More than 200 potential jurors summoned for trial of prosecutor in Ahmaud Arbery's death
item.description: A judge is dismissing some of the more than 200 potential jurors summoned for the trial of a prosecutor accused of meddling with the investigation of the killing of Ahmaud Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Winds picking up in Southern California and at least 2 new fires break out
item.description: Winds picked up on Tuesday in Southern California and at least a couple of new wildfires broke out as firefighters remained on alert in extreme fire weather two weeks after two major blazes started that are still burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
